name: Ahmose I
description: Ahmose I, the founder of the Eighteenth Dynasty, stands as a pivotal figure in Egyptian history. He is celebrated for expelling the Hyksos, foreign rulers who had dominated Lower Egypt for over a century. Ahmose's reign marked the end of the Second Intermediate Period and ushered in the New Kingdom, a golden age of Egyptian power and prosperity. Through strategic military campaigns, including the siege of Avaris, the Hyksos capital, Ahmose reunited Egypt under native rule. His military prowess extended beyond the Hyksos, with campaigns in Nubia and Palestine solidifying Egypt's borders and re-establishing its dominance. Ahmose's accomplishments were not limited to warfare; he also initiated significant building projects, restoring temples and revitalizing the Egyptian economy. He is remembered as a strong and decisive leader who laid the foundation for the subsequent expansion and cultural achievements of the New Kingdom pharaohs. Ahmose I is a symbol of Egyptian resilience, national pride, and the restoration of Ma'at (order and truth) after a period of chaos.
subtitle: Liberator of Egypt and Founder of Dynasty
achievements: Launched military campaigns in Nubia and Palestine

keyMyths.title: The Expulsion of the Hyksos
keyMyths.description: Ahmose I waged a long and arduous war against the Hyksos, finally defeating them at Avaris and driving them out of Egypt. This victory marked the end of foreign rule and the beginning of the New Kingdom.

keyMyths.title: The Nubian Campaigns
keyMyths.description: After securing Egypt's northern border, Ahmose I turned his attention south, launching campaigns into Nubia to reassert Egyptian control and secure valuable resources.
keyMyths.source: Inscription on the Temple of Karnak

keyMyths.title: Rebuilding Egypt
keyMyths.description: Following the expulsion of the Hyksos, Ahmose I initiated a period of reconstruction, restoring temples, commissioning new monuments, and revitalizing the Egyptian economy.

sources.author: Manetho
sources.description: An Egyptian priest who wrote a history of Egypt in Greek during the Ptolemaic period. His account provides a chronological framework for Egyptian rulers.
sources.text: An Egyptian priest who wrote a history of Egypt in Greek during the Ptolemaic period. His account provides a chronological framework for Egyptian rulers.

sources.author: James Henry Breasted
sources.description: A collection of translated ancient Egyptian texts, including inscriptions and autobiographies that provide insights into the reigns of pharaohs like Ahmose I.
sources.text: A collection of translated ancient Egyptian texts, including inscriptions and autobiographies that provide insights into the reigns of pharaohs like Ahmose I.

sources.author: Donald B. Redford
sources.work: Egypt, Israel, and Canaan in Ancient Times
sources.description: A historical analysis of the relationships between Egypt, Israel, and Canaan, providing context for Ahmose I's military campaigns.
sources.text: A historical analysis of the relationships between Egypt, Israel, and Canaan, providing context for Ahmose I's military campaigns.
